title_en: AI has taken over the stock market. The bond market is next
title_zh: 人工智能已接管股市，债券市场即将接力
rubric_en: Judging credit risk of the AI boom is difficult
rubric_zh: 判断人工智能繁荣的信贷风险并不容易

paragraphs.en: THE RAPID spread of new technological infrastructure is usually accompanied by booms in bond markets. The emergence of a liquid market for corporate credit on both sides of the Atlantic in the 19th century reflected the express growth of the railway industry. Before the arrival of railway operators with their vast investment needs—to buy land, lay tracks and ship goods—corporate finance was the province of small banks. Afterwards it became coupled with bond markets, at first nationally, then internationally.
paragraphs.zh: 新兴技术基础设施的快速扩张通常都会伴随着债券市场的繁荣。19世纪大西洋两岸企业信贷市场的出现，反映了铁路产业的迅猛增长。在铁路运营商以前，随着铁路运营商需要巨额投资——购买土地、铺设铁轨和运输货物——以前企业融资主要是小型银行的专属领域。随后，企业融资与债券市场相结合，先是在国内，随后才走向国际。

paragraphs.en: The latest techno-infrastructure frenzy, this time over artificial intelligence, is likewise stoking a bond-market boom (see chart 1). Since January Meta, Nvidia and Oracle have launched individual bond offerings worth $25bn apiece to fund their AI ambitions—more than any of them has ever raised in equity. So has SpaceX, which added the same sum to the $86bn that Elon Musk’s rockets-to-robots business raised from stock-market investors last month. In March Amazon, whose computing cloud hosts lots of AI workloads, sold $37bn in bonds. Alphabet issued a rare 100-year bond worth £1bn ($1.4bn) in Britain in February as part of a £5.5bn debt sale, plus more paper in Swiss francs, days after it raised $20bn at home.
paragraphs.zh: 这次新的技术基础设施狂热围绕人工智能，也在同样推动债券市场繁荣（见图1）。自一月以来，Meta、英伟达和甲骨文都分别发行了价值250亿美元的债券，以支持其人工智能布局——这一金额均高于它们历来通过股权融资所筹集的任何资金。SpaceX 也采取了同样做法，在该公司最近一个月从股市投资者那里募集到864亿美元火箭与机器人业务资金的基础上，又新增了同样数额的债务。3月，承载大量 AI 计算负载的亚马逊发行了370亿美元债券。谷歌母公司 Alphabet 二月在英国发行了一张罕见的100年期债券，规模为10亿英镑（约14亿美元），并将其纳入5.5万亿英镑债务发行的一部分；几天前，该公司刚在美国本土融资200亿美元，随后又发行了更多瑞士法郎计价债券。

paragraphs.en: Last year issuance of corporate bonds in America was not far off the all-time high set in 2020 (see chart 2). This year that record will almost certainly be broken, thanks in large part to AI. Morgan Stanley, an investment bank, expects $350bn-400bn in AI-related investment-grade issuance this year in American bond markets. That would be nearly a fifth of the $2.3trn in high-quality dollar-denominated bonds which companies are forecast to sell there in 2026. Another $50bn may come from junk bonds linked to AI projects (out of total high-yield issuance of $440bn).
paragraphs.zh: 去年美国公司债发行规模并不远离2020年创下的历史高位（见图2）。今年这一纪录几乎可以肯定会被打破，而其中很大一部分要归功于人工智能。投资银行摩根士丹利预计，今年美国债券市场的AI相关投资级债券发行规模将达到3500亿至4000亿美元。这将接近企业在2026年计划在美国发行的高质量美元计价债券总量（2.3万亿美元）的近五分之一。另有约500亿美元可能来自与AI项目相关的垃圾债（在总计4400亿美元的高收益债发行中）。

paragraphs.en: Such sums explain why the Bank for International Settlements, central banks’ central bank, recently warned that AI projects may not make enough money to repay the debt that financed them. If bond investing is the “negative art” of choosing what to avoid rather than what to buy, as it is sometimes described, how much avoiding must bond investors do?
paragraphs.zh: 正是这些规模解释了国际清算银行——各国中央银行的中央银行——近期为何发出警告：人工智能项目可能赚不到足够的钱来偿还为其融资的债务。如果债券投资被形容为“反向艺术”，即更像是在选择回避而不是选择购买，那么债券投资者应该回避多少才够？

paragraphs.en: In some ways, the AI-issuance bonanza makes the corporate-bond market appear a little safer than it was. The total debts of America’s five “hyperscale” cloud giants—Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft and Oracle—climbed by $228bn in the six months to March. That is nearly five times more than any such two-quarter increase in the past.
paragraphs.zh: 在某些方面，AI发行潮让美国企业债券市场看起来比过去更安全了一些。到3月为止的六个月里，美国五大“超大规模”云计算巨头——Alphabet、亚马逊、Meta、微软和甲骨文——的总负债增加了2280亿美元。这一增幅几乎是过去任何一个连续两个季度增幅的五倍。

paragraphs.en: The first four of the hyperscalers have for years churned out more profits than they know what to do with, and so enjoy strong credit ratings. Microsoft’s debt is considered safer than Uncle Sam’s. Only Oracle, the smallest and least profitable of the five, receives a middling “B” grade from big credit-rating agencies (though so do the issuers of about half of American corporate bonds these days).
paragraphs.zh: 前四家超大规模企业多年来赚得太多，利润过剩，所以享有较高的信用评级。微软的债务甚至被认为比美国国债还安全。只有甲骨文，这五家公司中规模最小、盈利能力最弱的一家，在大型评级机构那里得到的是中等的“B”级评级（不过如今大约一半美国公司债发行人也都拿到这种等级）。

paragraphs.en: You might expect a debt-raising boom to raise the spreads on corporate bonds, a measure of their riskiness relative to safe Treasuries. But thanks to the creditworthiness of the largest big-tech issuers, average spreads remain at around 0.8 percentage points, close to the narrowest they have been in a quarter of a century.
paragraphs.zh: 人们本来可能预期，融资发行潮会推高企业债利差，而利差是与无风险国债相比衡量债券风险的一项指标。但得益于最大发电商巨头的信用状况，平均利差仍维持在约0.8个百分点，接近过去二十五年来最窄的水平。

paragraphs.en: Investors are also beginning to be more discerning between the safe debt of the hyperscalers and bonds funding some of the riskier projects with which the tech giants are associated. In April QTS Data Centers, a firm owned by Blackstone, a giant manager of private assets, issued $4.6bn in bonds to fund an immense server farm in Georgia, of which Microsoft will be a client. Initially the bonds’ yield was 1.1 percentage points higher than for the equivalent Microsoft debt. Since then the spread has widened to 1.6 points (see chart 3).
paragraphs.zh: 投资者也开始更有分辨力地区分超大规模企业的安全债务，与这些科技巨头相关联的一些更高风险项目所对应的债券。4月，私人资产巨头贝莱德旗下公司 QTS Data Centers 发行了46亿美元债券，用于建设一个巨大的佐治亚州服务器农场，而微软将成为其客户。起初，这些债券的收益率比同等微软债券高出1.1个百分点。自那时起，利差已扩大到1.6个百分点（见图表3）。

paragraphs.en: On the surface, the ballooning issuance of riskier debt looks like a bigger cause for concern. The wave of junk bonds, set off last May by a $2bn bond offering from CoreWeave, which rents out top-tier chip capacity, is swelling. In addition, CoreWeave and fellow “neoclouds” like Nebius and Iren have all issued billions of dollars in convertible bonds this year, which investors can swap for a certain number of the issuer’s shares. The division between safer and riskier debt may be misleading, however. Historically, financial instability has tended to build up around assets that investors believe to be safe, not those they already see as sketchy. In this light, the junk bonds are a sign of market adaptation.
paragraphs.zh: 从表面上看，风险更高的债务发行激增似乎是更值得担忧的原因。由提供顶级芯片算力租赁服务的 CoreWeave 于今年5月发行的 20 亿美元债券所引发的垃圾债券浪潮正在不断膨胀。此外，CoreWeave 与像 Nebius、Iren 这样的“新型云服务商”今年也都发行了数十亿美元的可转换债券，投资者可将其兑换为发行公司的一定数量股票。然而，把债务简单划分为更安全和更危险两类可能具有误导性。历史上，金融不稳定往往往往围绕投资者认为“安全”的资产累积，而不是围绕那些他们已经视为可疑的资产展开。换句话说，这些垃圾债券反而是市场正在适应的一种表现。

paragraphs.en: When railway defaults surged in the wake of the financial panic of 1873, this eventually led to a worldwide depression. No such cataclysm looks imminent today. It is clear, though, that avoiding duds will be harder than ever. The AI revolution is proceeding at such a blistering pace that some early winners have already turned into losers, then, occasionally, back into winners—and vice versa. Divining which companies will come out on top, and be able to redeem their bonds when they mature in ten, 30 or, in Alphabet’s case, 100 years’ time, will require bond investors to be at their most negatively artful. ■
paragraphs.zh: 当1873年金融恐慌之后铁路违约激增时，最终酿成了全球性萧条。如今看不出会出现类似的灾难。但有一点很明确：筛出“雷”，比以往任何时候都更困难。AI 革命正以惊人的速度推进，一些早期赢家已经变成输家，然后又偶尔反过来变回赢家，反之亦然。要判断哪些公司会脱颖而出，并在10年、30年，或在 Alphabet 的情况下，是100年后债券到期时，能够偿付其债务的公司，需要债券投资者动用最顶级、最谨慎的判断力。■

paragraphs.en: For more expert analysis of the biggest stories in economics, finance and markets, sign up to Money Talks, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter.
paragraphs.zh: 如要获取更多关于经济、金融和市场重大事件的专家解读，请订阅《Money Talks》，这是一份每周发布且仅面向订阅者的通讯。
